content:

iPhone. Well done ! All other features have been well done but bit by bit with each update. Constant  updates have improved your app significantly . Thanks to your developers who update frequently fixing bugs. Appreciated !

It does everything I want it to do and was about to buy another copy for a family member but not at £29.95. It looks as if I’ll have to look for another player that is less expensive. It’s a real pity as I really like Evermusic. Bill

author: The best app I’ve found
title: Not bad…not great
content: The app is just okay and nothing more (and I have the premium version) Using two basic features like gapless playback and the equalizer together at the same time do not work correctly because transitions between songs are not smooth like they should be, 10 seconds before a track ends it cuts off for a second before the next track starts. I’ve tried changing to the core audio player but that is even worse as it skips through tracks in the playlist. I’ve asked for this to be fixed about 5 months ago but it still hasn’t. This app could be so much better but all the focus goes on fixing the wrong things.

author: Dekzcash

author: Pinkertonx
title: Excellent FLAC player
content: I’ve been looking for some time for a player on my iPhone and iPad that would work with my Bluesound music server and at last I’ve found one. Evermusic connects with my FLAC files uploaded to iCloud and works seamlessly. The offline function enables me to play my music on my iPhone whenever I’m away from home.  It’s a great product and well supported by experts. Top marks all round.

author: randy7021
title: Fantastic app
content: The only app that offers this much playlist and play-next customisation. The file uploader is great as well.

author: Kyladoll
title: slow
content: I used to love this app so much, I’ve even paid for premium due to how much I love it but recently it’s so insanely slow even taking minutes to add a song to a playlist. Some buttons don’t work and honestly its so bad

author: jmwd_4
title: Phenomenal - Only thing I use
content:
I’ve been using this app since 2021 and it’s honestly a godsend. Frequent updates, no subscription - just an optional one time payment for a couple extra bits (e.g. unlimted playlist and queue size) and I’m pretty sure it was only a tenner (although I payed it about 2 years ago so I’m not sure whether that’s changed), simple yet effective, equalizer is great, customisable in the right ways so you can just make the app do your own thing, and it just does what you need - no more and no less. 
Personally I download the songs myself into my phones files, and then move them to evermusic, so I’ve never explored to see if I could actually have just done it through the app and saved myself hours but hey that’s on me, not the app. And even without that sort of access it still functions fantastic just as an audio player. It’s not trying to do things you don’t want or need, which I appreciate. 
If you’ve got the time and patience to spare just getting the music yourself, you’ll save yourself thousands in music subscriptions, and if for some reason this app isn’t for you, at least it cost you nothing but a little time. And of course if you do love it like me, let the developer know! 
Thanks for this, it’s the best I could ask for.

author: Drago2299
title: One of the best
content: Brilliant app.

author: Mayco235
title: Brilliant
content: Only the best

author: Sweatybetty4me
title: Superb Free Music Player, Works Well With Sonos/Airplay/Bluetooth Speakers
content: I have a vast number of albums in MP3 format on a NAS (WD MyCloud) which I have struggled for years piping it through Plex (because of song number limitations in Sonos library) playing through Sonos and more recently trouble with a more than useless updated Sonos app. Constantly losing Plex connections, trouble with MyCloud and Plex “talking” to each other etc. This music app/player/organiser seems to be the answer to my prayers, why did I not spot it years ago? It will find all your albums on the MyCloud NAS and through the magic of “Airplay” play them all through my 12 Sonos speakers (tip: link your none airplay Sonos speakers to an Airplay enabled Sonos speaker).TBH, so far, the free version with some ads has been terrific and for just 99p per month, if you want the Premium version without ads, it seems a no brainer! No need to download any albums, songs onto my iPhone etc it will just play them from where they sit on my NAS, no mess!

author: Bartek Seroc
title: Was very good
content: Was very good till many songs stopped playing, couldn’t download it anymore because failed appears. Had to have spotify to play. I was more happy with Evermusic but doesn’t play many of my songs or started to cut other ones.

author: BlueGlassBoy
title: Truly game changing tech for audiophiles (with one request!)
content:
This app has revolutionised my listening and audio planning - its versatility, simple design and intelligent planning all make for a great experience. Having seen many of the early glitches get worked out and smoothed off has been a real treat to behold. I have an audio library of some 40k+ titles, and a very particular way of cataloguing and enjoying them, so Evermusic has been terrific.

I do have one continuing request however: some way of contains and dividing up Playlists. The playlist function is excellent but, if anything, too simple - I have masses of playlists based on Mood or season or personal preference, and they are becoming unwieldy. Some sort of ‘Playlist Folder’ option would be very greatly appreciated, to help organise those playlists. 

Keep up the great work!

author: Neil3365549
title: Data Harvester
content:
I didn't like how this app scraped an email address from my phone without my permission - and who knows what else 
I also found it awkward that iTunes music wasn't integrated into the library but instead resided in its own sub folder
Otherwise, looks good. It's a shame I have to delete due to lack of trust.

author: SearbyM
title: Plays well from iCloud
content: I had some teething troubles setting up this app but the help I got from their support service did the trick and now works perfectly.  This was the only app I found that will stream direct from iCloud.  Although the interface is a bit unimaginative it nonetheless works very well.

author: Chrisssaaa
title: Good solid reliable- once you get it to work
content: I am a fan of offline music because they are trying to zap me the whole time with invisible rays. No, not really! Anyway this player is great, been using it for a few years but it took several visits to my local computer shop to actually get my collection of ripped purchased CDs across into the player. Once they’re in it works great, has never let me down.

author: Bren611965
title: Why are are you trying to get more money out of me when I have already paid for premium?
content: Why are are you trying to get more money out of me when I have already paid for premium?

author: Barryo2003
title: Simply brilliant
content: I store music on my G-Drive and have been using Evermusic to stream to my phone and car using Apple Car Play for the last 6 months or so. It’s probably the best app I own. Borderline magical! Happy days. :)

author: uchennaezeh
title: Very Nice Music App
content: If you need a nice app to play audios you have saved in your iPhone's files and folders, this is it.

author: Av_ar
title: Scammm Trying to access to my bank app and trying to get my email pass
content:
Beware of this app . 
Trying to access to my bank app and trying to get my email pass each time message pop up says Evrmusic want access to you email or bank app
 This scam

author: Geoff4TC
title: A superb program
content: I have tried several of these cloud player apps and this is by far and away the best.   I have uploaded all of my vinyl disks, tapes and CDs onto the cloud server and I am listening to some of my music for the first time in many years.   Excellent reproduction, caching to ensure the player doesn’t pause for buffering, and a good indexing and playlist interface.   Excellent!

author: jmsehrrsn
title: Not as good as the reviews
content: This app is frustratingly inconsistent. While it performs adequately at times, it frequently falters and feels overly complicated, lacking a clear purpose. It attempts to emulate various platforms like Dropbox, Apple Music, and Spotify, but fails to excel at any. The interface is subpar, feeling like an afterthought rather than a well-designed feature. Connectivity with Dropbox, in my experience, is unreliable, often resulting in playback issues such as buffering and interruptions, even on a functioning fiber broadband connection. Despite meticulously organising album covers and artwork in relevant folders, the app no longer displays them as it did in the past, indicating a lack of ongoing development and maintenance. It's a shame because the concept had promise, but the execution falls short.

author: IanWarn
title: Awful App
content: This app really is bad. Only plays certain songs in a Playlist. Editing audio tags is useless. After paying for this I would expect some customer service but that is also useless. A complete waste of money.

author: Spudder2410
title: Good but could be better...
content: Hi, I love this app. But... Just a few tweaks needed. 1, it needs to be able to scan designated folders in the background. At the moment it seems to only add them when you are in the app. 2, it would be nice to have on/offline for favourites. 3, the app is downloading 2 of the same track when you have it downloaded in favorites and as a full album as well. And finally it doesn't seem to be able to stream most of my hi-res flacs. It stops and starts repeatedly, very choppy so I have to download them. It's the same on my MacBook too. None of my other apps have this problem and I have 1gb broadband. But other than that I love it ❤️

author: Bad Fr0g
title: Proper good
content: It’s proper good

author: Sy-87
title: App Problems and Ads
content: Moved to Cloudbeats which is better in comparison. Evermusic downloads stuff onto my files without my knowledge then plays music and then I find some of my music deleted in my google files and missing songs

author: aym_133
title: Exactly what i was looking for
content: I needed an app where i could transfer all my mp3 sound files into so i could listen to them like spotify and the like and couldn’t find a good one up untill now. exactly what i was looking for definitely recommend

author: 😎hello😎
title: It’s good
content: It’s good since it stopped working. My files mp3 audios are not exporting to the app but before all that the app was really good. Only one file exports when you select all the files. This app is a huge lifesaver and I can listen to my fave tracks offline but please fix the exporting issue when you select more that one file and only one file exports. Thanks

author: 55tyres
title: Excellent music player
content:
I’ve checked out numerous music player apps and Evermusic is by far the best. It’s simple & straightforward to connect to cloud services and any other music sources on my iPad. It’s fully featured with offline playback, auto sync, playlists & even a built in equaliser. The design & layout is easy to navigate and intuitive.
I have only one minor quibble: Crossfade playback is fine but not suitable for all albums. I have many albums where songs segue into one another and crossfade spoils the transitions. Hopefully gapless playback will be included in future versions.
I’ve told friends about Evermusic and those who’ve tried it agree with me: It’s the best music player available. Highly recommended.

author: AndyDu10
title: CarPlay needs work. STILL.
content: UPDATE 1: you say that you’ve fixed this, well you haven’t. CarPlay is still rubbish. When I change the equaliser settings on my phone and save them they do not update to the car. Sort CarPlay out it is rubbish and I have paid £10 for this app, if it doesn’t get sorted I’ll ask for a refund from apple. When using CarPlay and trying to connect it to my iPod list (1400 songs, iPhone XS Max) it sometimes comes up with a blank screen as if it can’t pull the songs through. Other times it does pull them through. Also, changing the green slider buttons (enable/disable) doesn’t always show up on the button (although it seems to apply the setting). Needs some work and then this will replace my best other player for in the car. Too many buttons looks cluttered. Should have the option for user to select which buttons they want and hide the ones they don’t use. I’ve paid £9.99 for this and it really needs work done. Not very happy with its issues.

author: BenAJN
title: Close to great
content:
Evermusic is a pretty solid app once you've got to grips with it but the are many, many failures of clarity from a UX standpoint. It's extremely unclear when a given action has had an effect or whether it's failed. The user is forced to investigate the matter themselves. If the action has failed the user then has to deduce why and how to fix the issue. No error message is given, no explanation, it's all just left to troubleshoot on the user end.

But all of that I can forgive to some extent. I don't know the size of the team that worked on this app. What I can't forgive is paywalling "offline mode". In other words, making the user pay to download files from their own cloud services, something the user could do simply by navigating to the Files app.

I'm more than happy to support apps I use a lot, there's no need for those kinds of practices.

author: fyn0tf1
title: Great Music App
content:
Been looking for an App like this for years, came across it whilst surfing online.
At last there is a way to have playlists and Music Library without having to use iTunes which most of the time deletes all your music off the iPhone.
With the payment of £2.99 to upgrade to Pro is well worth it because it allows you then to download titles and playlists to your iPhone storage for offline playing when there is no WiFi of 3/4
This app is a must if you want to bypass itunes and play your own purchased music without the monthly premium of streaming services.

author: Euby7
title: Finally!
content:
I’ve been waiting for an app like this for a while! 
I’m an avid music collector, and have made dozens of playlists from music i’ve purchased from iTunes, but whenever you sync your phone to your PC, it wipes them, so i needed an alternative to using Dropbox’s basic music player and i came upon this app. The fact i can sync my dropbox account to it and play all my music i’ve ripped/recorded from CD’s and Vinyl’s is a dream come true, and the app works perfectly. I instantly signed up to the pro edition so that i could make all the playlists i need. 
Great product, very pleased!
